component.name: dopeIPTV
component.summary: Modern IPTV player for Xtream Codes with EPG

description.p: dopeIPTV is a clean, modern IPTV client for Xtream Codes providers. It offers an embedded mpv player, an electronic programme guide, movies and series with TMDB metadata, recording and timeshift, Chromecast support, multiple playlists, themes and translations.
The embedded player is now fully self-contained.

ul.li: Bundle libmpv's own dependencies (ffmpeg, libass, ...), so in-app video works on any machine - not only where a matching mpv/ffmpeg is already installed. Fixes a black or disabled player on, e.g., a fresh Ubuntu 24.04
Never crash if the graphics stack can't create the video surface - the app stays up and offers external playback
CI now verifies the player loads inside a clean container

description.p: Packaging fixes so the app "just works" on more systems.

ul.li: Embedded video now works across distributions - the Linux builds no longer ship their own OpenGL/Mesa stack, which had clashed with the host driver and left a black player on Ubuntu
AppImage runs with no libfuse2 install needed (static runtime)
External mpv/VLC launches correctly from the packaged builds
Flatpak no longer crashes on libmpv builds missing an option
Silenced the fontconfig warning spam on launch

description.p: Bugfix: the embedded player is now included in the packaged builds.

ul.li: Bundle libmpv under the exact soname the player loads, so the in-app video player works in the AppImage/.deb/.dmg (0.4.0 shipped it under the wrong name on Linux)
CI now fails the build if libmpv isn't bundled correctly
Built-in TMDB artwork - posters work out of the box, no account needed
Trakt cross-device watched sync for movies, episodes and whole series
New Watched category split into Local and Trakt
Favourites for movies and series, with a Channels/Movies/Series split and Trakt list sync
Watch Later markers, watched badges, and richer covers throughout
Prompt before recording a different channel than the one playing
